User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the Cyber Gladiators application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

role.description: User's role in the application (e.g., 'Teacher' or 'Student').

xp.description: Total experience points (XP) earned by the user.

Quiz.title: Quiz
Quiz.description: Represents a quiz created by a teacher.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quiz entity.

teacherId.description: Reference to the User (Teacher) who created the quiz. (Relationship: User 1:N Quiz)

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a question within a quiz.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

quizId.description: Reference to the Quiz this question belongs to. (Relationship: Quiz 1:N Question)

answerOptions.description: Array of possible answer options for the question.

correctAnswerIndex.description: Index of the correct answer in the answerOptions array.

timer.description: Timer duration (in seconds) for the question.

BattleRoom.title: BattleRoom
BattleRoom.description: Represents a battle room for a quiz.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BattleRoom entity.

quizId.description: Reference to the Quiz being played in this room. (Relationship: Quiz 1:1 BattleRoom)

roomCode.description: Unique code for students to join the room.

qrCode.description: URL for the QR code that allows students to join the room.

BattleParticipation.title: BattleParticipation
BattleParticipation.description: Represents a student's participation in a battle.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BattleParticipation entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User (Student) participating in the battle. (Relationship: User 1:N BattleParticipation)

battleRoomId.description: Reference to the BattleRoom the student is participating in. (Relationship: BattleRoom 1:N BattleParticipation)

malpracticeCount.description: Number of times the student attempted malpractice (e.g., tab switching).

definition.description: Stores user data. Includes user's role and profile information.
Stores quizzes created by teachers. Includes denormalized 'teacherId' for authorization independence.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: Unique identifier for the teacher who created the quiz.

definition.description: Stores questions for a specific quiz. Includes denormalized 'quizId' for authorization independence.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: Unique identifier for the teacher who created the quiz.

definition.description: Stores battle room information. Each room is associated with a quiz.

params.name: battleRoomId
params.description: Unique identifier for the battle room.

definition.description: Stores student participation data for a specific battle room. Includes denormalized 'battleRoomId' for authorization independence.

params.name: battleRoomId
params.description: Unique identifier for the battle room.

params.name: battleParticipationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the battle participation.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to support the Cyber Gladiators application, with a focus on role-based access control, quiz management, and battle room functionality. The structure prioritizes authorization independence and simplifies security rules, enabling robust and scalable operations. Specifically, it ensures Authorization Independence by denormalizing relevant authorization data (e.g., teacherId) to subcollections where needed. Also, this design achieves that all documents in the same collections has the same security posture which improves security rules and reduce complexity.

The paths are structured to clearly define ownership and relationships between entities. User data is stored in a path-based structure (`/users/{userId}`) for private data. Quizzes and related entities are nested under the teacher's user ID, following a hierarchical path for user-owned data (`/users/{teacherId}/quizzes/{quizId}`). This design allows for secure and efficient ownership-based security rules. This structure supports the required QAPs by segregating data based on role and access needs, ensuring that list operations can be securely performed.

**Authorization Independence and Denormalization:**

*   The `quizzes` subcollection under each `user` document denormalizes the `teacherId` to ensure that all quiz-related operations can be performed independently of the parent `user` document.
*   `questions` subcollection under each `quiz` document denormalizes the `quizId` to ensure that all question-related operations can be performed independently of the parent `quiz` document.
*   `battleParticipations` subcollection under each `battleRoom` document denormalizes the `battleRoomId` to ensure that all battle participation operations can be performed independently of the parent `battleRoom` document.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   The structure enables secure list operations by segregating data based on role and access needs. For example, only teachers can list quizzes under their user ID, while students can only access battle rooms they are participating in.

*   **Ownership**: By using paths like `/users/{userId}/quizzes/{quizId}`, we ensure that only the teacher (`userId`) can manage their quizzes, enforcing ownership at the data structure level.
